title: gastrocalyx ampullata
intro: Gastrocalyx ampullata, also known as the bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant that belongs to the family Lentibulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating gastrointestinal problems. Gastrocalyx ampullata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its potential healing properties.
